input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-07T14:22:48.184Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.new_str:

    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolResult.sessionLog: Fixing zero-tests exit code

result.detailedContent: Fixing zero-tests exit code

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
Hmm, it still returned exit code 1. The `--no-restore` run returned 0 likely because nothing was built. Let me check if this MSBuild property is actually supported. Let me look at the log file to understand the error better.

Actually, looking more carefully at the output:
- "Failed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 0, Skipped: 0, Total: 0" — zero tests discovered
- Exit code 1

The property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` might not be the correct property name. Let me check what the correct property/option is.

For Microsoft.Testing.Platform, the way to handle this is:


I can either pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the test runner (since 8 is the exit code for no tests found), or use an MSBuild property, but I need to verify the exact property name first by checking the log file. I'm not sure if that property name is accurate—let me search for the actual MSBuild properties available for Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The real solution is likely either using the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` flag or `--ignore-exit-code 8` when running `dotnet test`, though there might be an MSBuild property I'm overlooking.

The property I added didn't work. Let me check what the correct property or approach is. Let me search for the actual Microsoft.Testing.Platform options.

The correct approach for Microsoft.Testing.Platform is to use `--minimum-expected-tests 0` command line option. But for an MSBuild property, I need to find the right one.

Let me check if there's a way to pass this through MSBuild properties or through a runsettings file.

Actually, let me check the installed SDK/packages to find the right property.

data.content: Good — `--minimum-expected-tests` must be non-zero. The right option is `--ignore-exit-code 8`. Let me also find the correct MSBuild property for this:

data.content: Found it — the MSBuild property is `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments`. Let me update the `.csproj` properly:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-f77a49d32edf49ecb2bc183c7e69ecc0/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->\n    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>", "new_str": "    <!-- Exit code 8 = \"no tests found\"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->\n    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>"}

toolArgs.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
toolArgs.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

+++ b/tmp/sv-f77a49d32edf49ecb2bc183c7e69ecc0/TestProject.csproj
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
-    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (matches legacy VSTest behavior) -->
-    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
+    <!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat as success to match VSTest behavior -->

data.content:
**Fixed.** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). 

Added to the `.csproj`:
```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

This passes `--ignore-exit-code 8` to the test runner, telling it to return 0 instead of 8 when no tests are found. Your CI will now treat zero-test assemblies as success again, matching the old VSTest behavior.

Alternatively, you can pass it at the command line without modifying the project file:
```
dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8
```
